businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robots, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. In 2025, annual revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, achieving 20.1% gross margin in Q4 2025 (highest in 2 years). FSD shifted to a $99/month subscription model to maximize recurring revenue. Key catalysts are set for April 2026: Cybercab production start and Roadster unveil.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Reflects AI/robotaxi future value but deeply overvalued on current earnings

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026 EPS estimate of $2.25. Still 4x+ above growth stock avg of 30-40x. Prices in explosive robotaxi and energy growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 2025 revenue $97.8B, barely grew YoY. 2026 consensus expects 15% growth to $108.9B

keyMetrics.name: Auto Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down from 25%+ in 2022 due to price cuts and competition. Q4 showed recovery to 20.1%

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: Larger than Toyota, VW, GM, Ford, BMW, Mercedes, and Hyundai combined. Valued as AI platform, not automaker

keyMetrics.name: Annual Deliveries
keyMetrics.interpretation: First-ever annual delivery decline (-1.1%). UBS projects Q1 2026 deliveries at 345K, down 18% YoY

growthDrivers.title: Wedbush reaffirms Terafab as 'game changer' (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush called the Terafab semiconductor plant plan a 'game changer,' reaffirming Outperform and $600 target. AI5 chip small-batch production starts late 2026, full production in 2027

growthDrivers.title: European sales rebound after 13 months (Feb +11.8%)
growthDrivers.description: ACEA data shows Feb registrations up 11.8% YoY, first increase since Dec 2024. However, BYD grew 100%+ in the same period, intensifying market share competition

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $20-25B joint chip factory (3/22)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI will jointly build the Terafab semiconductor factory in Austin. Plans to produce vehicle chips and AI satellite chips in-house. 2027 production target

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April production + Roadster late April unveil
growthDrivers.description: Steerless fully autonomous robotaxi Cybercab production starts and next-gen Roadster unveil both scheduled for April. Two major catalysts concentrated in a short window

growthDrivers.title: Netherlands FSD approval expected 4/10, EU expansion
growthDrivers.description: Tesla expects FSD Supervised approval from Dutch authorities by April 10. If approved, EU-wide expansion possible by summer

growthDrivers.title: China sales surge (+35% YoY) + $2.9B solar equipment
growthDrivers.description: Jan-Feb 2026 China sales up 35% YoY. Negotiating $2.9B equipment purchase from Chinese firms for 100GW US solar production capacity

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA escalates FSD probe, regulatory risk rises (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA escalated investigation into FSD performance in fog, dust, and low-visibility conditions. Potential recall could impact robotaxi timeline

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4 billion miles of real-world FSD driving data. Competitors cannot replicate this scale of autonomous driving training data in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and other competitors use Tesla charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all vertically integrated. Cost advantage and rapid innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader, 100%+ growth in Europe. Rapidly catching Tesla with price competitiveness

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi in SF, Las Vegas. Pilot operations in Austin, Miami. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in autonomous ride-hailing commercialization. Over 200K paid rides weekly

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV market share with Ioniq, EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Tesla stock 'true believers' could be losing faith
recentNews.significance: Put/call ratio at 1.75, highest in 3 years. Options market signals downside risk exceeds upside over next 30 days

recentNews.headline: Korea Model S/X orders close 3/31, sunset officially begins
recentNews.significance: Model S/X discontinuation streamlines lineup. Resources can focus on Cybercab and affordable models

recentNews.headline: Musk announces Terafab chip factory in Austin
recentNews.significance: Tesla, SpaceX, xAI joint venture at $20-25B scale. AI chip self-sufficiency. 2027 mass production target

recentNews.headline: UBS slashes Q1 delivery forecast by 18% to 345K units
recentNews.significance: Reflects global EV demand softening, BYD/Xiaomi competition, and brand damage from Musk's DOGE controversy in Europe/China

recentNews.headline: NHTSA escalates FSD probe over low-visibility performance
recentNews.significance: Safety concerns in fog and dust conditions. Potential recall or regulatory restrictions could delay robotaxi rollout

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush Dan Ives at $600 calls 2026 Tesla's 'transformation year.' JPMorgan at $150 says valuation is 'disconnected from automotive fundamentals.' Morgan Stanley downgraded from Overweight to Equal Weight in Dec 2025

risks.title: Extreme Valuation Risk
risks.description: TTM P/E of 356x is 15x the S&P 500 average of 23x. Any shortfall in growth expectations could trigger sharp declines. Forward P/E of 172x is 4x+ above growth stock averages

risks.title: FSD Regulatory and Safety Risk
risks.description: NHTSA escalated probe, California denied robotaxi application. Regulatory barriers could significantly delay robotaxi commercialization

risks.title: Core Auto Business Stagnation
risks.description: First annual delivery decline in 2025, 1% revenue growth. ASP down 8%, auto margin at 16.3%. BYD, Xiaomi intensifying low-cost competition

risks.title: Musk Risk (Brand Damage)
risks.description: DOGE political activity, Twitter fraud verdict causing European Jan sales to plunge 45%. Brand damage directly impacting demand

risks.title: Competition Intensification (Uber-Rivian, Waymo)
risks.description: Uber's $1.25B robotaxi deal with Rivian. Waymo at 200K+ paid rides weekly. Tesla's robotaxi monopoly premium eroding

risks.title: Geopolitical Risk
risks.description: Iran crisis driving oil price surge, Nasdaq in correction territory (-10%). Macro environment unfavorable for high-valuation growth stocks

buyReasons.title: April Catalyst Concentration
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, Netherlands FSD approval, and Q1 earnings all concentrated in April, creating short-term momentum potential

buyReasons.title: Explosive Energy Business Growth
buyReasons.rationale: Megapack revenue doubled in 2025, now 12% of total revenue. High-margin recurring infrastructure revenue reducing auto dependency

buyReasons.title: Terafab AI Chip Vertical Integration
buyReasons.rationale: In-house AI chip production reduces Nvidia dependency. Full vertical integration for vehicle, robot, and satellite chips maximizes cost competitiveness

buyReasons.title: Oversold Territory Entry
buyReasons.rationale: RSI near 30, down 23% from 52-week high. Institutional investors (BlackRock, Vanguard) increased positions in Q3-Q4 2025

overallOpinion: April concentrates key catalysts: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, FSD Europe approval, Q1 earnings. Any upside surprise could trigger a short-term reversal
Terafab strengthens the AI chip vision, but NHTSA probe escalation and California robotaxi denial increase FSD commercialization uncertainty
P/E of 356x is 15x the S&P 500 average. With the auto business stagnating, both robotaxi and robots must succeed to justify valuation
Technically below 50-day SMA ($405) with RSI at 41, bearish trend, but holding above 200-day SMA ($345). $364-368 support zone is critical

investmentVerdict.summary: Rational to wait for April catalyst outcomes. Extreme valuation and regulatory uncertainty coexist with technical bounce potential from oversold conditions

sources.name: Techi.com
sources.description: TSLA live price, comprehensive analysis and forecast

sources.name: CNBC
sources.description: Put/call ratio surge, investor sentiment shift analysis

sources.description: UBS delivery cut, NHTSA FSD probe escalation

sources.name: Teslarati
sources.description: Korea Model S/X order cutoff, official sunset

sources.description: Terafab chip manufacturing plans detailed analysis

sources.name: Capital Street FX
sources.description: TSLA technical analysis, RSI, EMA, support/resistance levels

x.text: Tesla $386, down 23% from ATH. Terafab chip factory announced, Wedbush holds $600. But NHTSA escalates FSD probe + UBS cuts deliveries -18%. April: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe. P/E 356x. Hold consensus
thread: 1/4 Tesla $386, down 23% from $499 ATH. RSI 41, approaching oversold. Below 50-day SMA ($405) but holding above 200-day ($345). Bearish short-term trend
2/4 Bulls: Terafab $20-25B chip factory, Cybercab April production, Roadster unveil, FSD Netherlands approval (4/10), China +35%. Wedbush $600 target reaffirmed
3/4 Bears: NHTSA FSD probe escalated, CA robotaxi denied, UBS Q1 deliveries -18%, Europe Jan -45% (DOGE backlash), Uber-Rivian $1.25B robotaxi deal
4/4 Verdict: P/E 356x = 15x S&P avg. Wait for April catalysts. Hold. Long-term investors can DCA at $340-365 support zone

threads.hook: TSLA $386 Analysis: April is the battleground
threads.text:
Tesla at $386, market cap $1.43T. Down 23% from ATH but still at P/E 356x (15x the S&P 500 average of 23x).

April Key Catalysts:
- Cybercab robotaxi production starts
- Roadster unveil
- Netherlands FSD approval (4/10)
- Q1 earnings (4/28)

Bullish: Terafab $20-25B chip factory strengthens AI vision. China sales +35%, Megapack revenue doubled.

Bearish: NHTSA FSD probe escalated, CA robotaxi denied, UBS deliveries -18%, Europe Jan -45% from DOGE backlash. Uber-Rivian $1.25B robotaxi competition.

Technical: RSI 41 bearish, below 50-day SMA. 200-day SMA ($345) support holding. $364-368 near-term support critical.

Verdict: Hold. Wait for April catalyst outcomes. Long-term investors can DCA into $340-365 support zone.

thread: 1/3 TSLA by the numbers: $385.95, P/E 356x, Fwd 172x. 52-wk range $214-$499. RSI 41, below 50-day SMA $405. 2025 revenue $97.8B (+1%), EPS $1.08

witty.hook: Tesla: AI dreams vs reality check
witty.text: Tesla P/E at 356x. 15x the S&P 500 average. At this price you're not buying a car company, you're buying 'robots driving the future.' Problem is NHTSA just hit the brakes on that future
thread: 1/3 Tesla market cap $1.43T = Toyota+VW+GM+Ford+BMW+Hyundai combined. But deliveries fell for the first time ever. This isn't a stock, it's a belief system
2/3 Musk: We're building Terafab for AI chips! Market: +3.5%. Same week NHTSA: FSD unsafe in fog. Market: -12%. Welcome to TSLA ownership
3/3 April has Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, earnings all at once. It's the all-in round. Hold and grab popcorn

empathy.text: Down 23% from $499 ATH. Put/call ratio at 3-year high. NHTSA probe plus delivery cuts. Tough stretch, but April catalysts are concentrated. Don't rush decisions. Let the data come in
thread: 1/3 If you bought at $400-450, this hurts. Three straight weeks of decline, put/call at 3-year highs. Market sentiment is at its worst, but that's also historically where bounces start
2/3 April brings Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe approval, Q1 earnings. Any upside surprise could flip the narrative. This is a patience game right now
3/3 Long-term: consider DCA at $340-365. Short-term: watching from the sidelines until Q1 earnings is wise. Whatever you choose, stay rational. You've got this

fact.text:
Tesla $385.95, mkt cap $1.43T, P/E 356x (15x S&P 500 avg of 23x).

2025 results: Revenue $97.8B (+1%), EPS $1.08, deliveries 1.79M (-1.1%). Auto margin 16.3%.

Q1 2026 outlook: UBS deliveries 345K (-18% YoY), EPS $0.41.

Key events: Terafab $20-25B chip factory (3/22), NHTSA FSD probe escalation (3/19), Model S/X Korea sunset (3/31).

April: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, FSD Netherlands (4/10), Q1 earnings (4/28).

33 analysts: Buy 12, Hold 13, Sell 8. Avg $399, range $25-$600.

Technical: RSI 41, below 50-day SMA ($405), above 200-day ($345). Support at $364-368.

Verdict: Hold. Await April catalyst outcomes.

witty.text:
Tesla's P/E is 356x. That's 15x the S&P 500 average. To justify this valuation, robotaxis, robots, energy, AND AI chips all need to hit. In poker terms, you're waiting for a royal flush.

Market cap $1.43T is bigger than 7 major automakers combined. But deliveries fell for the first time. This isn't a car stock, it's an 'Elon Musk Universe' ETF.

This week: Terafab announcement +3.5%, next day NHTSA FSD probe -5%. Owning TSLA is an unlimited rollercoaster pass.

NHTSA questions FSD in fog, California denies robotaxi, UBS cuts deliveries -18%, yet Wedbush shouts $600. Analyzing this company gives your brain a split personality.

April is the all-in round. Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, Q1 earnings all at once. Hold and watch with popcorn.

empathy.text:
From $499 ATH to $386. A 23% decline is never easy to watch. Put/call ratio at 3-year highs tells you the whole market is anxious.

NHTSA FSD probe, European sales plunge, delivery cuts... when bad news piles up, that's when it's hardest. But historically, emotional decisions during these periods cause the most damage.

April has major catalysts lined up: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, FSD Europe approval, Q1 earnings. Once these results come in, the path forward becomes clearer.

Terafab chip factory, China +35% growth, Megapack doubling - there are positive signals too. Not everything is doom and gloom.

Long-term investors: consider DCA at $340-365. Short-term: watching until April earnings is prudent. Right now, staying rational is your greatest asset. Hang in there.
